english: Fruit juice concentrate drink reduced sugar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'fruit juice drink' class; the 'less sugar' variant is not separately coded. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers fruit nectars and fruit drinks broadly; no specific less-sugar sub-code exists. Closest defensible match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for fruit juice drink (less sugar) as a food product; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving is one glass of approximately 250 ml, equivalent to 250 g, providing approximately 50 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Fruit juice drink (less sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo record for 'Fruit juice drink (less sugar)', CR ID D03070006. All per-100g values preserved as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — fruit juice and fruit drink category
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 'fruit juice drink (less sugar)' in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 55 is estimated by analogy to commercially diluted fruit juice drinks with moderate sugar content (4–6 g/100 ml), which cluster in the GI 50–60 range in published tables. GI 55 is classified as low (threshold: low <55 boundary inclusive per scoring model). Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (55 × 5.24 × 2.50) / 100 = 7.2 g. Input record GI of 55 (confidence 0.55) is consistent with this analogous estimate and is retained.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content (4.7 g/100 g) with negligible dietary fibre (0.25 g/100 g) and absent potassium data; sugar-sweetened beverages promote insulin-driven sodium retention and endothelial dysfunction.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage and substitute plain water, unsweetened herbal tea, or low-sodium sparkling water, as the added sugar content — even in a 'less sugar' formulation — contributes to insulin-mediated sodium retention and elevated blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 4.7 g sugar/100 g and GI ~55; liquid carbohydrates bypass satiety mechanisms and cause rapid postprandial glucose excursions, which is contraindicated per ADA 2024 Standards of Care.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should not consume this beverage; even the 'less sugar' formulation delivers rapidly absorbed liquid sugars that cause postprandial glucose spikes, and the ADA recommends eliminating all sugar-sweetened beverages regardless of sugar reduction claims.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages, including reduced-sugar variants, is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased visceral adiposity, and heightened cardiovascular risk per AHA dietary guidelines.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit or eliminate this beverage, as habitual intake of added-sugar drinks — even at reduced sugar levels — is linked to dyslipidaemia and increased cardiovascular event risk; plain water or unsweetened beverages are preferred.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar with dietary fibre below 1 g/100 g and no positive anti-inflammatory nutrient factor (no omega-3, polyphenols, antioxidants, or cruciferous components); fails the RA positive-criterion requirement.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage as it provides no anti-inflammatory benefit and the added sugar may promote low-grade systemic inflammation; unsweetened green tea or plain water are preferable alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fruit juice drink (less sugar) is a commercially produced, ultra-processed beverage made from fruit juice concentrate reconstituted with water and sweetened with added sweeteners, formulated to contain less sugar than standard fruit juice drinks. It is widely sold in Singapore and catalogued in the HPB Foodo nutrient database (CR ID D03070006). Per 100 g it provides 20 kcal, 5.24 g carbohydrate (of which 4.7 g is sugar), 0.05 g protein, 0 g fat, 0.25 g dietary fibre, and 9 mg sodium. Despite the 'less sugar' label, this product remains a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) by nutritional classification because it contains added sugar and negligible fibre. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low boundary), and a standard 250 ml serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 7.2 g. Clinically, this beverage is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease because liquid sugars are absorbed rapidly, bypassing satiety mechanisms and causing postprandial glucose and insulin spikes. The 'less sugar' designation does not confer meaningful health benefits relative to standard SSBs in clinical dietary guidance. It is commonly consumed as a snack beverage and is widely available in retail and food service settings across Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (CR ID D03070006) with confidence 0.95 for all macros; exact match to the named food record. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.55) consistent with published fruit drink GI ranges.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source (D03070006); this is a water-based beverage with no fat-containing ingredients by formulation — confirmed zero. iron_mg is reported as 0.0 in the HPB source; confirmed zero per approved source. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients — naturally zero. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo record for this product (D03070006) and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for an equivalent less-sugar fruit juice drink as of 2026-04-16. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on commercially diluted fruit juice drinks in the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct measurement for this specific product exists. gi_category set to 'low' as GI=55 falls at the low/medium boundary and the scoring model classifies 55 as low. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 5.24 × 2.50) / 100 = 7.2 g per 250 g serving. Local-language names added for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 multilingual compliance for Singapore-catalogued food.
